summary: profile.jsx has 4 new issues: partner lookup failures are masked as "no restaurant", save failures show non-actionable errors, name validation is minimal, and missing role translations leak raw enum values. Writing to the live findings stream was blocked by sandbox permissions.

findings.title: Partner lookup failures are rendered as a real "no restaurant" state
findings.description: When `base44.entities.Partner.get(userData.partner)` throws, the catch block suppresses the error and the component later falls back to `profile.no_restaurant`. A transient API failure therefore shows the same UI as a genuinely unassigned account, so the profile displays incorrect data with no error signal.
findings.suggested_fix: Track partner-load errors separately from the unassigned case, show an explicit error/retry state for the restaurant section, and only render `profile.no_restaurant` when the user truly has no partner.

findings.title: Profile save errors discard the API response and give users no actionable feedback
findings.description: `handleSave()` catches every `base44.auth.updateMe()` failure but ignores the `error` object and always shows the same generic toast. Server-side validation failures and transient network errors are indistinguishable, so users cannot tell what to fix after a rejected save.
findings.suggested_fix: Extract a safe message or error code from the API response, surface it in the toast and/or inline under the field, and keep the generic fallback only for unknown failures.

findings.title: The form does not validate `fullName` beyond non-empty trimming before submit
findings.description: `canSave` only checks `fullName.trim().length > 0` before calling `updateMe()`. Any validation rule beyond "not blank" is deferred to the backend, so the form can submit values that are locally invalid and only fail after the round trip.
findings.suggested_fix: Add explicit client-side validation that matches backend rules for the name field and block submit with field-level feedback until the value is valid.

findings.title: Missing role translations fall back to raw internal role codes
findings.description: `getRoleLabel()` uses the raw `userRole` enum as its fallback label. If a translation key is missing or a new role is added, the UI will render internal values like `partner_owner` instead of localized user-facing text.
findings.suggested_fix: Use a human-readable fallback map for known roles and reserve raw enum values for logging/debug output only.

missing_tests: Restaurant lookup failure should keep the section in an explicit error state instead of rendering the same copy as an unassigned account.
Profile save should surface server validation errors distinctly from generic network failures.
The name field should reject invalid input locally before `updateMe()` is called.
Missing role translations should fall back to readable labels rather than raw enum identifiers.
notes: The bundle contained no existing BUGS.md entries and no README.md context, so there was nothing additional to de-duplicate against.

I attempted to append findings to `C:\Dev\menuapp-code-review\.pipeline\runs\task-260317-231230-profile\artifacts\streams\codex-reviewer.jsonl`, but the sandbox denied writes outside the worktree (`UnauthorizedAccessException`).
No additional source files were read beyond the provided review bundle.
